response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some low-latency systems experience, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. While the BS plus 15+ years of experience satisfies the formal education requirement, the core technical domain mismatch is fundamental rather than a gap that could be bridged quickly at principal level.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization, GPU workload analysis, or SIMD/MIMD background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from performance architecture for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture experience
General performance and low-latency systems background
